mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed 0% in >60% of periods where UNRATE > 8.0%, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term assets combined with falling federal funds rates indicate liquidity expansion that fuels equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening economic output that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during expansion phases with Rank IC < 0.01 or forward returns turn negative in >55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window or housing starts fail to correlate with positive equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration above 0.4% monthly triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation months or the inflation threshold shows no statistical edge in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms broad-based economic strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform baseline with p-value > 0.05 or interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth signals economic contraction that reduces corporate profit expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during recessionary periods.
failure_condition: Signal predicts positive returns in >50% of recessionary periods or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the wrong direction.
